experiments: Calculate important numbers based on data sets using Python modules and build predictive functions (small dataset).
Acquire and explore big data sets: data distribution, normal and random distributions, scatter plots.
Implement a back‑propagation artificial neural network and test with appropriate data sets.
Apply Bayes’ rule in Python to compute the probability of student absence given it is Friday.
Implement k‑Nearest Neighbour algorithm to classify the Iris data set.
Implement simple linear regression algorithm reading training data from a CSV file.
Implement logistic regression (binomial) algorithm reading training data from a CSV file.
Implement naïve Bayes classifier for a CSV training set and compute its accuracy.
Implement decision‑tree ID3 algorithm and use it to classify new samples.
Perform k‑means clustering with three centroids on given data and predict class for a new instance (VAR1=0.906, VAR2=0.606).
